images.prompt:
[SUBJECT] A vertical split-frame architectural cross-section: on the left half, a tall tech-project tower rising upward through stacked strata labeled by geometry — a transistor-lattice ground plane, a logic-gate mezzanine, an instruction-set deck, a compiler gallery, a framework atrium, a model-weights floor, an uppermost agent-level platform — each floor load-bearing for the next and dimmed into primitive-floor hatching below the current working surface; on the right half, a humanities-project shaft descending downward through inverted strata — a consciousness cap layer, an experience layer, a language layer, an embodiment layer, a culture layer, a material-conditions basement — each successive layer reframing the one above it as artifact rather than ground; between the rising tower and descending shaft, a wide horizontal non-meeting gap fills the center of the frame with fine sediment-hatching and small suspended artifact-fossils, marking the two excavations as oriented-toward-each-other but never closing.

Foreground Color: #CA8F31 industrial yellow main color in foreground

Background: Flat black #000000

A vertical split-frame architectural cross-section rendered as a single wide comparison plate. Left half: a tall tech-project tower rising upward through clearly demarcated stacked strata labeled only by geometry — a transistor-lattice ground plane, a logic-gate mezzanine with switching motifs, an instruction-set deck with register-files, a compiler gallery with pipeline schematics, a framework atrium with component-tree motifs, a model-weights floor with matrix hatching, and an uppermost agent-level platform. Right half: a humanities-project shaft descending downward through an inverted stack of strata — a consciousness cap layer, an experience layer, a language layer, an embodiment layer, a culture layer, a material-conditions basement — each layer drawn so the one above reads as artifact under the one below, using fine geometric reframing-ticks at each boundary. Between the tower and the shaft, a wide horizontal gap at the middle register of the frame is filled with dense sediment hatching and small suspended artifact-fossils at reduced weight, explicitly showing the two excavations as oriented-toward-each-other but not meeting.
